arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ قَيِّمُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ
transliteration: Allahumma lakal-hamd, anta qayyimus-samawati wal-ardi wa man fihinn
translation: O Allah, all praise is due to You. You are the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth and all that is within them.

insights.meaning: The opening of the Prophet's (SAW) night prayer supplication — an expansive praise acknowledging Allah's sustaining of all creation.
insights.benefits: Opens the heart for Tahajjud by centering the mind on Allah's greatness before making personal requests.
insights.when: Beginning of Tahajjud prayer, before qiyam.
insights.explanation: Ibn Abbas (RA) narrated that the Prophet (SAW) would begin his night prayer with this comprehensive praise, acknowledging Allah as the One who holds all of existence.

arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan waqina adhaban-nar
translation: Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

insights.meaning: One of the most comprehensive duas in the Quran — asking for goodness in both worlds simultaneously.
insights.benefits: Covers every blessing needed in dunya and akhirah in one beautiful supplication.
insights.when: Can be recited at any time, especially powerful in Tahajjud and during Tawaf.
insights.explanation: Anas (RA) said this was the dua the Prophet (SAW) recited the most. It encapsulates the complete aspiration of a believer in both worlds.
